paraphrase.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
paraphrase.body: Qatar Airways has announced plans to resume services to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting July 15, 2025. The airline confirmed that four weekly flights to Helsinki will be operated initially, increasing to daily services from August 1. Tokyo Haneda will also receive four weekly flights from the launch date. The expansion brings the airline's network to over 160 destinations, according to a statement from Qatar Airways. HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani praised the move, highlighting its significance for Qatar's connectivity.
key_facts: Resumption of flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15, 2025
Four weekly flights to Helsinki, increasing to daily from August 1
Four weekly flights to Tokyo Haneda from July 15

Opened with a varied lead: 'Qatar Airways has announced plans to resume services...'

factual_grounding.note: GROUNDING verification, NOT fact-checking. This confirms the draft's specifics (names, numbers, dates) trace back to the source brief. It does NOT verify the brief itself is true — if the brief is wrong, the draft can be perfectly grounded and still factually incorrect. Ungrounded specifics are items a reviewer must check before publish.
